title: Boat Rental in Tonga: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Planning a boating holiday in Tonga on a budget is entirely feasible, with day trips from a port like Neiafu in the Vava'u group starting from around €150 per person. You can compare verified boat listings and book directly through day charters in Tonga to find options that suit your wallet.

Where to set off in Tonga

The main departure points for boat hire are concentrated in the Vava'u island group. Neiafu, the main port, has several marinas including the Vava'u Marina and the Moorings base. Further south, the harbour at Pangai on Lifuka island in Ha'apai is quieter but offers access to pristine reefs. In the Nuku'alofa area, the main wharf and the small boat ramp at Faua Harbour are used by local operators. For day trips, the calm waters inside the Vava'u caldera are ideal.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tonga?

Typical prices range from €150 for a shared tour to €1,500 per day for a crewed catamaran. The total depends on the boat size, season and duration. For a half-day hire of a small motorboat suitable for four people, budget around €300. A full-day catamaran for up to ten guests costs about €800 in low season. Crewed monohulls with skipper start from €600 per day. These figures exclude fuel, which is extra and can cost €50, €100 for a day's cruising.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (Bowrider or centre console): Best for fishing and exploring nearby coves; seats 4, 6 people; from €250 half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42): Ideal for families or groups; comfortable for 8, 10 guests; from €700 full-day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Fairline 43 Targa): A sporty choice for a faster cruise around the islands; carries up to 8 guests; from €900 per day; for more details see the Fairline charter page.
  4. Crewed monohull yacht: Perfect for a luxury overnight trip with a skipper; sleeps 6; from €1,200.

Costs and budget breakdown

  • Shared group tour per person: from €150 (includes snorkelling and lunch)
  • Private small motorboat half-day (up to 4 guests): €250, €350
  • Catamaran full-day charter (up to 10 guests): €700, €1,100
  • Crewed yacht with skipper full-day (up to 8 guests): €600, €1,500

When to go and do you need a licence?

Peak season runs from June to September, when the trade winds are steady and visibility is best. The prevailing wind is the south-easterly trade wind, blowing at 15, 20 knots. Regarding licences, if you plan to skipper a boat yourself, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is generally accepted, but the operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. For bareboat charters, a sailing qualification or experience log may be requested.

If you anchor overnight in the Port Maurelle area, be sure to check the tide: the depth can drop by over a metre within a few hours.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: Half-day (4, 5 hours) or full-day (8, 10 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 people
  • Currency used: EUR (local currency is Tongan pa'anga, but most charter firms quote in EUR for international bookings)

Questions about boat rental in Tonga

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Tonga?

Yes, if you hire a boat with a skipper, no licence is needed; for self-drive boats an ICC or equivalent is typically required.

What is the best island to visit by boat from Neiafu?

Swallows Cave and the surrounding islands in the Vava'u group are a classic day trip, located about 10 nautical miles from Neiafu.

How far in advance should I book for June 2026?

For the high season, booking at least two to three months ahead is advisable to secure your preferred boat.

Are there any hidden costs I should know about?

Fuel, mooring fees at some anchorages, and a cleaning surcharge (typically €20, €50) may be added to the base price.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, planning your trip through the BoatHire24 marketplace gives you access to verified operators. If you own a vessel in this stunning archipelago, you can also list your boat on the BoatHire24 marketplace to reach travellers worldwide. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Tonga or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tonga and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
